idea: STRUCTURAL: 1p2c split-output WN=256 TILE_M=128 STAGES=2, analogous to iter061 fp16 design that got 382T at 8192^3. 2 consumers compute rows 0-63 and 64-127 simultaneously, hiding each others TMA wait. SMEM=224KB fits in 228KB
expected_gain: +30% TFLOPS from 2-consumer overlap beating 1-consumer at same WN
